titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/123

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=597 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:09:40.302875+00 | translation moa=596 status=live modified=2021-04-07 08:09:43.617663+00

div.p:
WILLELMUS dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Episcopis . Abbatibus . Comit’ . Baron’ . Iust’ . Uicecom’ . Prepositis . Ministris . et Omnibus probis Hominibus
totius terre sue clericis et laicis  Salutem . Sciant presentes et futuri nos concessisse et Hac carta nostra confimasse deo et Sancte Marie et ecclesie de
Melros et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus In liberam et quietam et perpetuam elemosinam . ecclesiam de Hatstanesden’ . cum terris et decimis et omnibus
ad ipsam ecclesiam pertinentibus . ad susceptionem et sustentationem pauperum et peregrinorum ad domum de Melros uenientum  a Ioc’ . Glasguen’ . episcopo per consilium
nostrum assignatam . De qua ecclesia cum esset inter nos et eundem episcopum controuersia . ad quem nostram donatio ipsius ecclesie pertineret  ius nostrum quod in ea ha
buimus . uel habere potuimus  eidem episcopo concessimus . ad ipsam ecclesiam secundum deum ordinandam . Et ipse episcopus ut prouentus ipsius ecclesie in caritatis operibus expendantur  et in bonos usus perpetuo conuertantur  ipsam ecclesiam cum omnibus pertinentiis suis pro animabus omnium predecessorum nostrorum et suorum . et salute et anima nostra et sua . et omnium
heredum nostrorum . et successorum suorum . per consilium et concessionem nostram ad perpetuos usus pauperum et peregrinorum . ecclesie et conuentui de Melros pia consideratione contu
lit et confirmauit . Ut autem hoc pium factum et rationabile perpetuam et illibatam habeat firmitatem  predictam ecclesiam de Hatstanesden’ cum omnibus perti
nenciis suis . deo et Sancte Marie et ecclesie de Melros . In liberam et pereptuam elemosinam ad Sanctos usus prenominatos concedimus et Hac carta nostra confirmamus . Tenendam In
perpetuum ita libere . Quiete plenarie et Honorifice  Sicut aliqua parrochialis ecclesia in regno nostro liberius . Quietius . Plenius . et Honorificentius . ab aliqua conuen
tuali ecclesia tenetur et possidetur . Salua pensione viginti solidorum ecclesie Glasguen’ de Ipsa ecclesia de Hatstanesd’ ab ipsis monachis singulis annis persoluenda . Sicut
carta eiusdem Ioc’ . Glasguen’ episcopi testatur . Et ne inter nos uel heredes nostros uel ipsos qui de manerium predictum tenuerint . et ipsos monachos aliqua in posterum
super pastura predicte uille possit esse controuersia  per preceptum nostrum et per considerationem proborum Hominum nostrorum prouisum est . quantum predictum Monachi in pastura predicte
uille habere debeant . Scilicet ducentas matrices oues . et sexdecim boues . et quatuor uaccas . quod illis in pastura illa perpetuo Habend’  concessimus . et hac carta nostra
confirmauimus . firmiter prohibentes nequis eis super Hoc  iniuriam aut uexationem aliquam inferat . Super nostram plenariam forisfacturam . Test’ . Ioc’ . Glasg’ .
episcopo . Ric’ . Episcopo Morauen’ . Æ . abbate de Dunferm’ . Com’ . Dauid fratre nostro . Hug’ . Cancellario nostro . Willelmo de Lind’ . Iust’ . Phil de valon’ . Dauid de Lindes’ .
Hugone de Normanuill’ . Waltero de Berkel’ camerario nostro Herb’ . de Camera ___ Apud ___Melros

William by the grace of God king of the Scots to the bishops, abbots, earls, barons, justices, sheriffs, provosts, officials, and all worthy men of all his land clerk and lay sends greeting. Let present and future know us to have granted and by this our charter to have made firm to God and St Mary and the church of Melrose and the monks serving God in that place, in free and quit and perpetual alms, the church of Hassendean with lands and teinds and all that pertains to the church itself, assigned by Jocelin bishop of Glasgow by our advice for the reception and support of the poor and of pilgrims coming to the church of Melrose. Since there was a dispute about this church between us and the same bishop, to which our donation of this church pertains, we have granted our right which we had or could have had in it to the same bishop to be disposed to that church according to God. And the bishop himself, so that church’s harvest in the works of charity should be distributed and perpetually be converted to good uses has by pious regard conveyed and affirmed that same church with all that belongs to it, for the sake of the souls of all our predecessors and his own, both for the salvation and also the souls of us and him, and of all our heirs and his successors, by our advice and consent, for the perpetual use of the poor and of pilgrims, to the church and convent of Melrose. In order that this pious action, both reasonable and complete, should endure for ever we have granted and by this our charter affirmed the aforesaid church of Hassendean with all its pertinents to God and St Mary and the church of Melrose in free and everlasting alms for the afore-named holy uses; to be held for ever as freely, quitely, fully and honourably as any parish church in our kingdom is as freely, quitely, fully, and honourably held and possessed by any conventual church; saving a pension of twenty shillings to be paid each year by the monks to the church of Glasgow in respect of the church of Hassendean, just as the charter of Jocelin bishop of Glasgow bears witness; and lest there could be any dispute in the future between us or our heirs or those who shall have held of the aforesaid manor and those monks over the pasture of the aforesaid toun, it is provided by our command and by the consideration of our worthy men, we have granted and by this our charter affirmed that the aforesaid monks ought to have for ever in the pasture of the aforesaid toun, to wit, two hundred ewes, sixteen oxen, and four cows, firmly forbidding that anyone commit any injury or vexation to them over this, on pain of our full forfeiture. As witnesses: Jocelin, bishop of Glasgow; Richard, bishop of Moray; Archibald, abbot of Dunfermline; Earl David our brother; Hugh our chancellor; William of Lindsay, justice; Philip de Valognes; David of Lindsay; Hugh de Normanville; Walter of Berkeley our chamberlain; Herbert Chalmers. At Melrose.
